verses.text: Judgment is toward priests, people, and king, who have been 'a snare' to the nation. Leaders accused. Those who should have guided have ensnared instead.
Judgment is toward priests, people, and king, who have been 'a snare' to the nation. Leaders accused. Those who should have guided have ensnared instead.

God knows Ephraim's defilement; their deeds 'will not frame their doings to turn unto their God,' for pride blinds them. Hardened by sin. Pride keeps a people from the repentance that would save them.

God knows Ephraim's defilement; their deeds 'will not frame their doings to turn unto their God,' for pride blinds them. Hardened by sin. Pride keeps a people from the repentance that would save them.

God knows Ephraim's defilement; their deeds 'will not frame their doings to turn unto their God,' for pride blinds them. Hardened by sin. Pride keeps a people from the repentance that would save them.
They shall seek the LORD with flocks but not find Him, for He has withdrawn; they have begotten 'strange children.' Sought too late. God may withdraw when He is sought only in form, not in truth.
They shall seek the LORD with flocks but not find Him, for He has withdrawn; they have begotten 'strange children.' Sought too late. God may withdraw when He is sought only in form, not in truth.
Blow the alarm; Ephraim shall be desolate, and Judah's princes are like those who 'remove the bound'; on them His wrath is poured. Justice on all. Both kingdoms feel the stroke of God.
Blow the alarm; Ephraim shall be desolate, and Judah's princes are like those who 'remove the bound'; on them His wrath is poured. Justice on all. Both kingdoms feel the stroke of God.
Blow the alarm; Ephraim shall be desolate, and Judah's princes are like those who 'remove the bound'; on them His wrath is poured. Justice on all. Both kingdoms feel the stroke of God.
Ephraim is oppressed and broken; God will be to him 'as a moth' and 'as rottenness.' Slow decay. Sin eats away a nation as a moth wastes a garment.
Ephraim is oppressed and broken; God will be to him 'as a moth' and 'as rottenness.' Slow decay. Sin eats away a nation as a moth wastes a garment.
When Ephraim saw his sickness he turned to Assyria, who could not heal; God will tear and withdraw 'till they... seek my face.' Wrong physician. Only when men own their sin does God return to heal.
